hypothesis: Introducing aromatic residues at position 5 and hydrophobic residues at position 11 will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions with ECL2/ECL3 and TM5/TM6, while avoiding off-target (SSTR3/5) binding through steric complementarity. This will increase the selectivity margin by differentiating SSTR2 from off-target subtypes.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current designs are failing due to poor structural quality and insufficient disruption of off-target contacts. Tightening clash constraints and focusing mutations on non-pharmacophore positions will improve both structural quality and selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: Current candidates have poor structural quality (pLDDT=0.0), suggesting excessive clashes. Reducing the clash tolerance will enforce better geometry and improve structural quality.

To improve selectivity, we need to disrupt off-target contacts at non-pharmacophore positions. Focusing on these positions will allow us to introduce mutations that reduce off-target binding while preserving key pharmacophore residues (FWKT(7-10), Cys3/Cys14).
